plan_phases.description: Months 1–2: Redirect $450/month to retirement savings + $325/month to emergency fund
Months 3: Build emergency fund — $550/month + $225/month to retirement savings
Months 4–6: Redirect $450/month to retirement savings + $325/month to emergency fund

recommendations.title: Catch up on your tax reserve
recommendations.body: Your reserve is roughly $1,000 of an estimated roughly $1,350 target. You've started — staying on plan keeps you ahead of next quarter.
recommendations.next_move: Add roughly $350 before your next quarterly (due in 27 days).

recommendations.title: Build buffer during your stronger months
recommendations.body: Your income shows month-to-month variation. Earmarking part of stronger months builds buffer for the lean ones.
recommendations.next_move: When income comes in above your typical, redirect the difference to liquid savings before it gets reabsorbed.

recommendations.title: Tell us how you handle your freelance income
recommendations.body: A separate business account gives us more confidence in scoring your income volatility and tax-reserve target.
recommendations.next_move: Confirm in settings whether you use a separate business account or mix freelance with personal banking.

recommendations.action: Months 1–2: Redirect $450/month to retirement savings + $325/month to emergency fund
recommendations.reason: Your personalised plan: Months 1–2: Redirect $450/month to retirement savings + $325/month to emergency fund → Months 3: Build emergency fund — $550/month + $225/month to retirement savings → Months 4–6: Redirect $450/month to retirement savings + $325/month to emergency fund
You currently have 2.5 months of essential expenses saved. Three months is the minimum buffer to absorb income shocks.
At your age the target for your age is roughly $54,000; you're about 72% behind. Consistent contributions today compound into the next decade.
Life coverage is one of five resilience milestones; term policies are inexpensive at younger ages.

recommendations.action: Add disability insurance to your benefits
recommendations.reason: Disability is a top cause of unplanned income loss; check whether your employer offers it.
